summary: The primary concern is securing an open airway through procedures like endotracheal intubation before addressing infection or performing surgery; initiating antibiotic therapy should follow this critical intervention but remains essential throughout care due its role fighting systemic spread from infected tissues within oral cavity leading up towards head/neck region potentially causing severe complications requiring urgent attention even after stabilizing breathing issues caused initially by swelling blocking throat passage ways thus preventing adequate oxygen exchange between lungs & bloodstream thereby risking hypoxia especially among already vulnerable individuals suffering acute inflammation secondary bacterial colonization likely present given nature disease process itself involving deep spaces beneath tongue floor mouth etc...
